claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - Alex, who's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask: **Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Next question: **Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask: **Do you make regular financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep, such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation? If so, how much do you contribute?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep - I help with things like clothes, school expenses, and other bits and pieces. I give about £26 a week.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

To complete my assessment, I need to ask: **Has anyone else (such as the person Alex lives with) claimed Child Benefit for Alex, or do you know if they are planning to claim?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! Based on the information you've provided, you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement  
✓ You live in the UK  
✓ Even though Alex doesn't live with you, you're making regular financial contributions of £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep (clothes, school expenses, etc.)  
✓ Your contributions meet the requirement of paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after Alex  
✓ No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex  

According to the rules, you can qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child, which includes making financial contributions towards their upkeep that are at least equivalent to the Child Benefit amount. Since you're contributing £26 per week and no one else is claiming, you meet the eligibility criteria.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement. Although Alex does not live with the claimant, the claimant is making regular financial contributions of £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep (including clothes and school expenses). According to the rules, you can qualify for Child Benefit if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after the child. The current Child Benefit rate is approximately £24 per week for the eldest/only child, and the claimant is contributing £26 per week, which exceeds this amount. Additionally, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant qualifies as being 'responsible' for Alex and meets the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex (age 8). Although Alex doesn't live with the claimant, they make regular contributions of £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets or exceeds the Child Benefit amount. Since no one else is claiming for Alex, and only one person can claim per child, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

According to the rules, you can qualify for Child Benefit if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after the child. The current Child Benefit rate is approximately £24 per week for the eldest/only child, and the claimant is contributing £26 per week, which exceeds this amount. Additionally, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant qualifies as being 'responsible' for Alex and meets the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and has one child, Alex (age 8). Although Alex doesn't live with the claimant, they make regular contributions of £26 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which meets or exceeds the Child Benefit amount. Since no one else is claiming for Alex, and only one person can claim per child, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
